A0158.title: Formal Verification of Smart Contracts
A0158.definition: The use of mathematical methods to rigorously prove the logical correctness of a contract.
A0158.description: Use formal specifications, model checking, symbolic execution, or theorem proving during smart-contract development to mathematically verify critical asset flows, access control, state transitions, and boundary conditions, catching high-impact flaws such as reentrancy, overflow, and privilege bypass before deployment.
A0158.limitation: Formal verification is costly and depends on accurate specification modeling. It may not cover oracle behavior, cross-chain interactions, governance processes, or external systems, and verification results must be maintained as contracts evolve.
